applies_when: synthesis addresses balance between voices or hands

criteria.text: Names a specific voice or hand (melody / inner / bass / LH / RH).
References a specific bar number or time range (e.g., 'bars 3-6').
Prescribes a concrete dynamic or touch target (e.g., 'below mp', 'lighter').
Avoids vague balance language without specifics ('better balance', 'more musical').
Names exactly one actionable practice strategy (e.g., 'play LH alone').
Names which pedal phenomenon (over-pedaling, late change, blurred harmony, dry, flutter).
References a specific bar / harmony change / beat where the issue lives.
Prescribes a concrete pedal action (half-pedal, change on beat 3, release before next chord).
Connects pedaling to an audible musical outcome (clarity, resonance, color).
Includes a practice strategy (no-pedal first, listen-only pass, mark the pedal in score).

applies_when: synthesis addresses tempo flexibility or rubato

criteria.text: Distinguishes expressive rubato from unintentional drift.
Names where in the phrase the rubato lives (peak, approach, recovery).
References style or composer expectation for rubato use.
Avoids generic 'play with feeling' framing; prescribes a measurable shape.
Includes a practice strategy (metronome to freed, conduct the breath, sing the line).

applies_when: synthesis addresses musical sentence shape or phrase

criteria.text: Names at least 2 of {start, peak, resolution} of the phrase explicitly.
References a specific bar or beat where the peak / breath sits.
Connects phrase shape to a dynamic, agogic, or tonal-color decision.
Addresses the line as a unit (does not isolate individual notes).
Includes a practice strategy (sing the line, slur in groups, breathe at phrase end).

applies_when: synthesis addresses pulse, timing instability, or tempo evenness

criteria.text: Distinguishes drift (gradual) from rushing (acute) from dragging (acute) - not just 'uneven'.
Names where in the piece the instability appears (specific bars or transition).
Identifies a likely cause (technical demand, hand-coordination, breath, performance anxiety).
Prescribes a tempo target or a stable reference (metronome BPM, internal subdivision).
Includes a practice strategy (subdivision, slow-fast-slow, hands-separately at tempo).
Names the actual range observed (compressed, narrow, mostly mf, lacks pp, lacks ff).
References specific bars where the issue is sharpest.
Connects range to expressive function (rising arc, climax, surprise contrast).
Avoids 'play louder/softer' without target dynamic level or context.
Includes a practice strategy (extreme contrasts as exercise, listen for ceiling/floor).

applies_when: synthesis addresses note attack, release, or articulation

criteria.text: Names the articulation type at issue (legato break, staccato length, accent placement, slur shape).
References specific bars or a specific figure (e.g., 'the 16th-note runs').
Connects articulation to era/style expectation (Baroque clarity, Romantic blend).
Avoids vague 'clearer' framing; prescribes a touch or finger-level technique.
Includes a practice strategy (slow detached, dotted rhythms, hands separately listening).

applies_when: synthesis proposes a concrete drill or practice exercise

criteria.text: Names the specific skill the exercise targets (one dim or a specific gesture).
Specifies passage scope (exact bars / hands / tempo).
Specifies progression (start tempo / target tempo, or simple to complex chain).
Specifies a stop criterion (% accuracy, comfort, 'until even').
Avoids generic 'practice slowly' framing without measurable terms.
